text:
Two unlikely friends meet on a cross-country cycling trip.

One rainy afternoon, sitting beneath an old oak tree near a quiet creek, Alex pulled out his old leather book. It was filled with stories about faraway places. There was a chapter on medieval knights and dragons, another on medieval maidens who fell in love with talking eagles. Alex thought about reading it aloud. Something about adventure excited him.

Beside him was Sam. Sam was no adventurer; he was a retired librarian who once tried reading the ancient runes left by the spirits of the creek. He wore glasses that never fit quite right, which only seemed to complement his perpetual frown. Instead of a book, Sam had a fishing rod.

They had been traveling together for weeks. Each day consisted of cycling about twenty miles. They had met earlier that month during a bookstore sale. Alex found an old-fashioned diary behind a mountain of genre fiction. He loved writing it down, each day jotting something clever or philosophical.

As Sam
